title: Neochilenia chilensis
taxonomy: Neochilenia chilensis is classified within the family Brassicaceae. This family is commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family, and it includes a wide range of flowering plants. The genus Neochilenia is a relatively small group within the Brassicaceae family. Neochilenia chilensis is native to Chile and is a species of flowering plant. It is a type of cabbagewort or mustard plant, characterized by its distinctive flowers and growth habit.

intro: Neochilenia chilensis, also known as the Chilean yerba, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Neochilenia chilensis include sesquiterpenic lactones, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neochilenia chilensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and immune system function.
